records.title: How to Repair Rust on Your Car Without Welding (No Special Tools Needed)
records.problemAddressed: Body rust around wheel wells, rocker areas, and other sheet-metal trouble spots.
procedureSummary: Grind back the visible rust and loose paint.
Treat remaining corrosion and rebuild the damaged area with filler.
Sand, prime, and repaint to seal the repair.
warnings: This is appropriate for body rust, not structural frame loss.
Rust will return if active corrosion is trapped under filler or paint.

records.title: Brake pad replacement with ChrisFix and NRS Brakes
records.problemAddressed: Brake pad service and corrosion-resistant pad selection.
procedureSummary: Inspect pad thickness and rotor condition.
Replace worn pads and service the contact hardware.
Favor rust-resistant pad backing hardware for longer service life.
warnings: Brake work is safety-critical; do not skip slide and hardware inspection.
Truck braking performance changes quickly with towing or oversized tires.

partsUsed.notes: Source article focused on galvanized brake pads to resist rust.

records.title: ChrisFix sway bar bushing diagnostic reference
records.problemAddressed: Suspension clunk diagnosis around sway bar bushings and brackets.
toolsMentioned: hand tools for bushing bracket service
procedureSummary: Shake-test the sway bar near the bushing with the truck safely supported.
Inspect for cracking, looseness, or bushing size mismatch.
Replace worn bushings and hardware as needed.
warnings: Do not confuse sway bar clunk with ball-joint or control-arm play without inspection.
Wrong inner diameter or low-grade rubber causes repeat failures.
